name: Mountain Gorilla
conservationEfforts: Dian Fossey Gorilla Fund and Gorilla Doctors provide veterinary care. Tourism revenue funds conservation. Population has grown from 620 in 1989 to over 1,000 today.
description: Mountain gorillas live in the volcanic mountains of central Africa at elevations of 2,200-4,300 meters. They are one of conservation's greatest success stories, with populations slowly recovering.
